name: Thompson, McClellan
motto: "In politics he beats the best, Therein his success lies."
clubs: Mississippian '20-'21, Board of Control, Circulation and Assistant Business Manager '20, Business Manager '21
Y. M. C. A. Council and Cabinet, Secretary of Cabinet '19-'20
Clerk University Practice Court, Civil Term
description: "Mac" has always taken a great interest in student work, especially that pertaining to his profession. He will be particularly missed in the literary societies, and has done splendid work for the Mississippian. He leaves us with one of the highest honors, "President of the Graduates."

name: Berry, Sidney B.
motto: "His honors are honors that speak for themselves. Just look at his record, then judge for yourselves."
clubs: Secretary of Gordon Hall Boarding Club, '21-'22
Managerial and Editorial Staff Ole Miss, '21
Y. M. C. A. Cabinet '21, Council '20
description: "Sid," with his quick grin and his keen sarcasm, is known to us all for his happy-go-lucky air, but underneath lies a sympathy for his fellows that amounts to love. His record is more than enviable—a record few have equaled and none surpassed.

name: Mccain, Addie Elizabeth
motto: "Of noble mind, serene and kind."
clubs: President of Y. W. C. A., '20
Vice-President of First Year Law Class, '21-'22
description: The gentle murmur of the tiny stream trickling sweetly over its mossy bed, the sweet fragrance of the narcissus, the strength, the innate beauty, the silent majesty of a Grecian column, the purity of the whitest lily is Addie. A student, a worker, a friend, and, more than all, a true woman.

name: Haumont, Louis Paul Firmin
motto: "I am a man; I deem nothing human alien to me."
description: Ole Miss is not only glad to name Prof. Haumont among her faculty, but proud that his M.A. will be in her name. It is always an honor for Mississippi to number a man of France among her students.

name: Alonzo Philips George
motto: "A student, an athlete, and a man."
description: "Jap" has done so much for Ole Miss that we hardly dare mention one thing, for fear that something more important has been left out. For instance, just look through our athletic section. "Jap" did it.

name: M. Beckett Howorth
motto: "Dost thou love life? Then do not squander time, for that is the stuff life is made of."
clubs: National Committeeman of Alpha Phi Epsilon, '21-'22
description: The student body elected Beckett the busiest man on the campus, and many who know him best call him a genius. His record at Ole Miss is remarkable both for scholarship and for varied activities. He has a master mind accompanied with an insatiable thirst for endeavor, and great ambition linked with the highest and noblest ideals.

name: Wilberne McNease
motto: "Not over serious, not too gay, But a rare good fellow."
description: In addition to his literary work, "Mac," aspiring to the title of "Professor," has been teaching the Junior "Meds" what he learned of anatomy, and the majority have found it "too much for one man."

name: Farley, Robert J.
motto: "None but himself can be his parallel."
description: "Bob" left us to teach school last year, and is now back again to prepare for the bar. His return is quite an advantage for the literary societies, student publications, and his class.

name: Somerville, Lucy Robinson
motto: "Sincere and true to her own beliefs, With a brilliant original mind; A leader who's fearless and strong and just; A girl of the highest kind."
description: Lucy not only proved to the lawyers that a woman can excel in jurisprudence, but that some women have executive ability and leadership. This makes us observe that one doesn't have to be great of body to be great of mind.

name: Tubb, Thomas Jefferson
motto: "With his real sharp tongue and his ready wit, As a lawyer he can't keep from making a hit."
clubs: President of the A. S. B., '20-'21
Y. M. C. A. Cabinet, Treasurer '20-'21
description: Tubb is proof of the old adage, "Every Tubb must stand on its own bottom," and he has shown himself perfectly capable of doing so. He has held the highest positions that the students can give; he takes away two degrees and a wealth of legal learning.

name: Hathorn, J. C.
motto: "He passes his hours among his books; You find it in his bleary looks."
description: "Happy's" election as best student in 1922 leaves no doubt as to his ability and to the fact that he is a worker. He helped us down A. and M. in baseball, and is always mixed up in the important campus happenings.

name: Smith, Wilburn Suber
motto: "The hand that follows intellect can achieve."
description: Preaching takes up so much of Smith's time that out of classes he is rarely seen at all. A friend to everyone, and a student equal to any.

name: Mebane, Annette
motto: "Ever gentle, and so gracious with her learning."
description: Annette is another co-ed who is successfully braving the test of a Doctor's degree, and is easily proving that woman can accomplish things as easily as man.

name: Robert, William Pierre
motto: "Shortly his fortunes shall be lifted higher; True industry doth kindle honour's fire."
description: After Pierre received his "Lit" from A. and M., he discovered that no medicine could be learned among the cows and poultry, and so left his home to come to Ole Miss and learn one of the best of professions. We are fortunate in having him again next year.

name: Luther, Leslie Bryson
motto: "As proper a man as one may see on a summer's day."
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Bryson is a man to be depended upon, whether it be in class or student activity. Tennis players find him a hard match.

name: Buchanan, F. J.
motto: "It is not good that man should live alone."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: "Buck" left us last year to take up household duties in Oxford, but he has not let this education interfere with his studies.

name: Dailey, Louis Irving
motto: "A lion among ladies is a most dreadful thing."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: "Tubby," our Beau Brummel from Torrance, studies law, but practices the "fair sex." A staunch exponent of Rockefeller's method of getting rich.

name: Davidson, Philip Grant Jr.
motto: "He is complete in feature and mind, with all good grace to grace a gentleman."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: An untiring worker, but fortunately afflicted by a severe case of "Rixitis." Philip has aided materially in upholding our football standard.

name: Draughn, Paul Vernon
motto: "Turn, Fortune! turn thy wheel with frown or smile."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Since Paul was not "found" by any of the campus publications, he decided to edit something himself, and his results with the Third Floor News tend to place him in the genius class.

name: Edwards, Clyde Beaman
motto: "He knows enough who knows how to live and keep his own counsel."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Clyde doesn't try to display his good qualities. One has to know him to appreciate his good worth.

name: Elliott, Lucy Caldwell
motto: "I love her for her smile, her looks, her way of speaking gently."
major: Bachelor of Science, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Always smiling, care-free and happy, we see Lucy. Eternally glad that there is a world to live in, and as glad that she lives in it, heartily disposing of her duties.

name: Farley, Alice May
motto: "Earth's noblest thing—a woman perfected."
major: Bachelor of Arts, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Though, many times, Alice May becomes the ever-present help in time of trouble, she is utterly unassuming of the faith and love which she so richly deserves. One of the most dependable and satisfying girls in the University.

name: Garner, Eugene Hendrix
motto: "Where duty calleth, he is always found."
major: Bachelor of Science, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Another Sargent S. Prentiss in the making. Hendrix will probably be the man to pick the all-American oratorical team in the near future. He is a man known for his good disposition.

name: Gregory, Roy Alfred
motto: "Great of heart, magnanimous, courtly, courageous."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Gregory gave up his educational work to come to us and take medicine. We predict that he will stand high in his profession.

name: Herring, G. B.
motto: "Of soul sincere, in action faithful and in honor clean."
major: Bachelor of Arts, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Already selected to assist in the School of Commerce, Herring has quickly shown business-like qualities, and in football his opponents had to get down to business.

name: Hume, Mary Ritchie
motto: "She is good as she is fair, none—none on earth above her! As pure in thought as angels are: To know her is to love her."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Someone expressed our thoughts in saying, "I wish that I had that in my life which gives Mary the look in her eyes." She is as pure as the dewdrops on the rosebud, as bright as the bloom.

name: Johnson, Vivian Annette
motto: "A strong mind, prompted by noble purpose,"
major: Bachelor of Arts, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Vivian has endeared herself to all those who have known her during her stay here, and those who will remain after she has gone, as well as those who go with her, will miss her smile and her helpful, attractive way.

name: Jones, Evern
motto: "In every deed he had a heart to resolve, a head to understand, and a hand to execute."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
clubs: Ole Miss Post Commander, American Legion, '19-'20
description: A prominent member of our "Ole Miss" American Legion and A. E. F. Jones always has been a leader in student activities. He is the type who goes after it and gets it.

name: Kitchell, Birdie
motto: "Those who know her best love her most."
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: A Louisiana maid whose Titian hair calls forth envy in the heart of all girls. Birdie is a friend of whom her friends can truly say, "A friend is God's masterpiece."

name: Lamb, Lois
motto: "A good heart is better than all the heads in the world."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Lois may be just a bit whimsical, but we know her to be as true to a friend as the needle to the pole, and as thoughtful of others as some of us are of ourselves.

name: Lipscomb, Joseph Dubois
motto: "My mind to me a kingdom is."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Joe has always been counted an asset in literary circles. He learned that a bunch of Georgia girls were going to Blue Ridge last summer, and decided that he could get in better work there than in Oxford.

name: Lemasson, Lena
motto: "She thinks without confusion clearly, Loves her fellowman sincerely, And acts from honest motives purely."
major: Bachelor of Arts, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Lena is a firm believer in the doctrine, "Man alone does not excel in books," and has added another argument against it—herself. Honesty, frankness, firmness and faithfulness, with knowledge gained from books, unite to make her a rare type.

name: Long, William Alexander
motto: "We don't want him any longer; He is Long enough already."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Cabinet and Council
description: "Bill," the "Boy with the Bullfrog Voice," has been elected the most popular man on the campus, has made the Mississippian one of the South's greatest college papers, and is responsible for the Lyceum's success.

name: Mccall, Geraldine
motto: "Original wit and heart of gold, Charming personality and grace, we're told."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Geraldine has always been a winner as one of the co-eds' basketball team, and for unselfishness, friendliness, clear thinking, cleverness and popularity no one could find a better.

name: Meek, John Tarble
motto: "English I love, Math I adore, But ladies-away with them forevermore."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Meek would not have been elected most bashful boy if one co-ed could have stuffed the ballot box. How could he keep such late hours and be like that!

name: Metts, Dewey M.
motto: "Better late than never, but better never late."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Metts intends to teach after he leaves us, and can be relied on to make the most of his profession. He is a practical man, not the kind to theorize.

name: Middleton, Benjamin F.
motto: "Drink wine and live here blitheful while you may! The morrow's life too late is—live today."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, School of Arts and Sciences
description: "Ben" is well on the road to success as a theatrical man through his performances with the Marionettes, and he "plays his part" well at Ricks.

name: Mills, Newton Webster
motto: "Blest with temper, whose unclouded ray Can make tomorrow cheerful as today."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Mills reminds us of our childhood days, always asking for a nickel—but when we were kids we never returned one. He is a hard worker.

name: Moody, Margaret
motto: "'Tis well to be merry and wise. 'Tis well to be honest and true."
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, School of Arts and Sciences
clubs: Vice-President Woman's Student Government Legislative Council
description: Our only regret is that we had Margaret with us only two years, for in her we found a real girl—one who struck a happy medium between work and play. Sincere, lovable and capable—according to Dame Rumor, she is quite an authority on the "Jap" situation.

name: Crawford, William Burton Potts
motto: "The heroes are not all six feet tall; Large souls may dwell in bodies small."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: One of those quiet, unobtrusive persons who has slipped through his four years and is slipping out again without most of us knowing him; but those who do know him can speak well of his school work and will hate to lose him.

name: Price, Emy Lou
motto: "When she has passed it seemed like The ceasing of exquisite music."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: We predict for Emy Lou much success in life. She was equally popular with both boys and girls, and always kept several of the former "on her string." A good student, conscientious and true.

name: Price, Ouida
motto: "What she wills to do or say Seems wisest, virtuousest, discreetest, best."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: In Ouida are combined those qualities of power, of patience, of thoughtfulness, duty, strength and reserve which make that fairest and best of all God's creations—a true woman. No one could be more.

name: Richmond, Lois Elizabeth
motto: "And music, too, dear music! that can touch Beyond all else the soul that loves it much."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: A wealth of fun, humor, good fellowship and ability, all directed by one clear and quick-thinking brain, and all the property of their proud possessor, Lois. No peer in her realm! We will be glad if sunny Tennessee will send us more like her.

name: Rogers, George Winters
motto: "The elements so mixed in him that nature might stand up And say to all the world: This was a man."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Phi Sigma numbers George among her best, and he has taken a leading part in many student activities. He will make good use of his training as a future member of the Mississippi bar.

name: Scott, Roy
motto: "Soft peace she brings, whenever she arrives,"
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Vivacious, gay, a symbol of the great outdoors is Roy. Where laughter is merriest, good comradeship predominates, and behind all an earnestness and courage, there she will always be found.

name: Sisler, Ella
motto: "A countenance in which do meet Sweet records, promises as sweet."
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Behind her camouflage of a quiet, exclusive manner, a protection from the curious public, there is the real Ella. As sincere as a lover, as sweet as the honeyed drop collecting within an exquisite flower, and with a gentle personality which gives one the thrill of a garden landscape.

name: Sisler, Ruth
motto: "So sweet a face, such angel grace In all this land has never been."
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: When we look at Ruth we are carried back to the days of Achilles and Troy, when mighty warriors fought for fair maidens. And instead of blonde or brunette, many of our boys voted her their type of girl.

name: Smith, Elizabeth
motto: "You best the thought canst raise, the heart attune, Light as the busy clouds, calm as the gliding moon."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: The ideals for which she strives are always the highest; no jollier comrade nor truer friend can be found - no, not even after a longer search be made.

name: Short, Ethel Gladys
motto: "She is pretty to walk with, And witty to talk with, And pleasant, too, to think on."
major: BACHELOR OF ARTS, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: In all the fair Magnolia State no fairer flower could be found. At the piano she has no peer. Gladys is a wonderful pal, the same every day, no matter where you see her. When we want something done we call on Gladys.

name: Spratlin, Oran Dewey
motto: "He hath indeed bettered expectations."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: Another Ole Miss student who will make his life work that of imparting his knowledge to others, and whom we think will be a worthy representative of the profession.

name: Thompson, Robert W., Jr.
motto: "He was a man, take him all in all, And I shall not look upon his like again."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
clubs: Secretary, Critic, Vice-President Hermaean Literary Society
Chairman Ole Miss Cotillion Club Dance Committee, '21
description: A faithful follower of the school of Archimedes. Robert's presence will be missed in every field of college activity, as he has been an acknowledged leader in literary, social and executive functions.

name: Unger, Anel Merton
motto: "Not to enjoy one's youth when one is young is to imitate the miser who starves beside his treasures."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: At the outset the ambition to teach Sophomore English became Unger's ruling passion, and his intense interest in this alone has deprived most of us of the opportunity to see what would have been the results of his efforts in other lines.

name: Waugh, Edgar Wiggins
motto: "This above all: to thine own self be true, and it must follow as the night the day, thou canst not then be false to any man."
major: Bachelor of Arts, School of Arts and Sciences
clubs: Representative of University in Mississippi Intercollegiate Oratorical Contest, '21
description: One fellow who doesn't waste his time, or at least you never see him doing it. Waugh's real worth began to be known through his "Cynic's Ban," but, before and since, he has performed creditably his every duty.

name: Williams, James M.
motto: "Consistency? I never changed my mind, Which is, and always was, to live at ease."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: "Jimmy" came to us from Annapolis, convinced that he didn't like sailing, and we hate to lose him. He thought he could play football, and so came out and showed us he could, instead of telling us what he had done.

name: Williamson, Pauline
motto: "That continuous sweetness which with ease Pleases all around it, from the wish to please."
major: Bachelor of Science, School of Arts and Sciences
description: Pauline came to us with a subtle charm and a delicacy of manner, a delightful personality which has gradually grown upon us and enveloped us in the warm glow of her generous and unselfish heart.

name: Wilson, William Lawrence
motto: "For he, while his companions slept, Was toiling upward in the night."
major: BACHELOR OF SCIENCE, SCHOOL OF ARTS AND SCIENCES
description: "Bill" hails from Meridian, and that city considers him an able representative. We know him as a cheerful, regular fellow and a busy student.

name: Boyd, A. N.
motto: "Perseverance conquers all things."
description: Though he comes to us from Texas, Boyd doesn't pack a pair of "pluggers." We don't wish our sister state any hard luck, but we wish we could get more like him.

name: Brown, John E. Jr.
motto: "Still they gazed, and still their wonder grew That one small head could carry all he knew."
description: While Buster has the distinction of being Ole Miss' youngest medical student, he has succeeded in upholding the standards of his class, and we think he will not let those of his profession lag.

name: Campbell, George Alexander
motto: "On with the dance! Let joy be unconfined; No sleep till morn, when youth and pleasure meet."
description: George, the pride of Kosciusko, entered the university as the greenest freshman and has developed into one of her prime Terpsichorean artists. But, laying jokes aside, he has taken his medical work seriously and ranks well in his class.

name: Cross, J. B.
motto: "He who does not know his way to sea should take a river for his guide."
description: One of the quiet and unobtrusive kind who believes in deeds rather than words, and sticks to his professional work rather than being a candidate for "degree in diversity."

name: Cleveland, Joseph Dewey
motto: "Night after night he sat and bleared his eye with books."
description: Cleveland is not the kind to flaunt his qualifications before the public view, but his associates know him as most capable, and his fellow students placed him at the head of the Medical Club.

name: Culpepper, J. P., Jr.
motto: "When night hath set her silvery lamp on high, then it is time for study."
description: Here is another believer in the eternal triangle, but his is Gordon Hall, the Medical Building, and the Coop (hypotenuse between Gordon and the Coop).

name: Eversberg, Charles Edward
motto: "But chiefly the mold of a man's fortune is in his own hands."
description: Another Texas man who prefers Mississippi schools, and came here to get his laurels. Eversberg will take back with him a touch of the quality of Mississippi.

name: Farrish, George Clayton
motto: "Give me the moonshine, Give me the girl, And leave the rest to—chance."
description: "Red" bats .400 in the good-fellowship league and plays "hands" in his famous "duet." A good all-round man and one worth knowing.

name: Feemster, Lucien Carl, Jr.
motto: "Modest Carl, from the realms of science, Hath reached this position by diligent appliance."
description: Carl sets a high mark in his class which any "Med" would find hard to beat. We are sorry that his profession has taken so much of his time away from us.

name: Frashuer, W. E.
motto: "Blessings on him who first invented sleep."
description: We understand that Frashuer hibernates during the summer, as he has to get up for a class now and then during the rest of the year. Why not nickname him "Pullman"? He intends to make sleeping sickness his specialty.

name: Holladay, Perry Ellington
motto: "Had he been caught when young, He might have been tamed."
description: "Aleck" missed his vocation when he tackled medicine. Seems that a man who is a typical walking advertisement would have made his profession some branch of the School of Commerce. (But we forget the Volstead act.)

name: McDaniel, Bennie Osborne
motto: "While then the world's mine oyster, Which I, with sword, will open."
description: Someone once asked someone else why there was a man on the campus who was called "Possum," and that someone was asked if he'd seen him. He hadn't. The next day he reported, "I have, and he is." "Possum" will be sorely missed when the followers of the pigskin reassemble in 1923.

name: McIntyre, Daniel Roy
motto: "A medical student of fame am I, If my pills can't cure, you surely will die."
description: Much cannot be said of "Mac," for he has said it all himself. But even if he does, he doesn't exaggerate.

name: Montelepre, Philip
motto: "What is man, if not a mystery."
description: It will probably be never explained why "Monty" keeps the facial alfalfa, but from reports at Columbus we learn that it had an effect on M. S. C. W. which was disastrous—to the girls. But we will always attribute it to his "fiddle." Fiddlesticks!

name: Oberschmidt, Charles McCormick
motto: "Honors achieved far exceed those that are created."
description: Besides his medicine, he is at his best on the pawing end of a piano. If he can cure like he can play, look out, patients!

name: O'Mara, Braxton Bragg
motto: "True as the needle to the pole, Or as a dial to the sun."
description: A few years ago he thought he was through, but now he's back to finish his education—Medical and RICKity. He tells us that he is to specialize in bone-surgery, as he has the head for it. He's a large man, but worth his weight in gold.

name: Reynolds, Joe S.
motto: "I care for nobody, no, not I, If nobody cares for me."
description: He comes to us from Meridian, and if he "hangs out his shingle" in his home town, she will have something to be justly proud of. He doesn't tell you about it; he just does it.

name: Robertson, May Clare
motto: "Those about her shall read the perfect ways of honor."
description: To one who can penetrate beneath her veil of dignity May Clare reveals a fund of sweetness and honesty which will be thoroughly appreciated by many a fair patient, and which has endeared her to those so fortunate as to be numbered among her friends.

name: Smith, Reuben Milton
motto: "Aye, every inch a king."
description: Arkansas lost a good man when she sent Smith to us. If Arkansas were made like that—well, we'd like to live in Arkansas. We hope that Mississippi will not lose what she has gained.

name: Strange, Will Wright
motto: "The man who consecrates his hours By vigorous effort and honest aim."
description: It wouldn't be Strange if some member of the Senior Medical Class should fail to represent them well. "Will" is quiet and likable, and can hardly fail to succeed.

name: Wood, Charles Spencer
motto: "Work, courage, honor, these indeed Your sustenance and birthright are."
description: Spencer came to us as one of the famous Batesville Baseball Trio, and without him we couldn't have won the last two pennants. Between baseball and classes, Spencer has time to get in a little "Foot"-work.

name: Woodson, Turner Ashby
motto: "If the rascal hasn't given me medicine to make me love him, I'll be hanged."
description: Ashby's characteristic smile is famous for making co-eds' hearts go "potato-potato." Ashby wanted to give the other boys a chance, or he might have again headed the list for most Apollo-like features this year.

name: Allen, Joseph Franklin
motto: "A lion among ladies is a most dreadful thing."
major: BACHELOR OF LAW, School of Law
description: The boy from Kosciusko, takes to work like a duck to water. His ambition is to represent his district at the capital city. With those piercing eyes and thin, long curling lashes, he will realize his dream.

name: Reece, O. Bickerstaff
motto: "Life, what art thou without love?"
major: BACHELOR OF LAW, SCHOOL OF LAW
description: "Bick" is such a jolly good fellow he really must be in love. With his cheerful smile, he wins a place in your heart. For all that, a serious student.

name: Brewer, John Money
motto: "Is this that haughty, gallant, gay Lothario?"
description: A more courteous lawyer cannot be found. Brewer is a fine student and has made a host of friends with his cordial manner. "The elements so mixed in him that nature might stand up and say to the world, 'This is a man.'"

name: Brooks, Clorus G.
motto: "Not over-serious, not too gay, but a rare good fellow."
major: BACHELOR OF LAW, SCHOOL OF LAW
description: Brooks returns to Lake with a thorough knowledge of law and the assurance that he has pursued justice in every case. His community has a right to be proud of his achievement.

name: Wilkes, H. Davis
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man. Who dares do more is none."
major: School of Law, Bachelor of Law
description: If you would select a man of sterling qualities, Davis would be your choice. He denounces injustice with keen sarcasm, regardless of the consequences, and is a speaker of unusual ability.

name: Ernest, Austin Erwin
motto: "He would hide his talents under the bushel of his modesty."
major: School of Law, Bachelor of Law
description: Erwin comes to us from Tennessee, with the zeal for work customary with all Tennesseans. There is dignity in his manner, which makes his words effective before the jury.

name: Murray, C. Falkner, Jr.
motto: "They say thou art a lawyer in disguise. 'Tis well, I would not suspect it otherwise."
major: School of Law, Bachelor of Law
description: Never worry, never hurry, Jack will get there yet. But Falkner always speaks to the point, argues forcibly, reasons clearly and logically, ruling out irrelevant and immaterial matter.

name: Finch, John Sidney
motto: "I am sure care's an enemy of life."
major: Bachelor of Law, School of Law
description: Finch came to us from Cumberland Law School, and has duplicated here the high record he made there. Where he got his smile we do not know; he came here with it, and has had it ever since.

name: Hathorn, Carl Manar
motto: "The bed has become a place of luxury to me; I would not exchange it for all the thrones in the world."
major: Bachelor of Law, School of Law
description: Indifferent in manner, "Judge" surprises his opponents with his ready wit. If he can resist the corner store, he will make a fine lawyer.

name: McGee, Sidney Earl
motto: "A bold, bad man."
major: Bachelor of Law, School of Law
description: McGee is the man who would not hurry through life in a day, but slowly and surely accomplishes his bit, smiling as he goes over the rough places.

name: Leonard, B. Melvin
motto: "If hot air were music, I would be a whole brass band."
major: Bachelor of Law, School of Law
clubs: Winner Mississippi Pine Land and Planter's Scholarship, '18, '22
description: Melvin, possessed with a voice like a preacher, realizes the fact that a lawyer earns his pay by talking. He is conscientious and public-spirited, and will always be among the leaders in his community.

name: Denton, James Thomas
motto: "Hear ye not the hum of mighty workings?"
major: Bachelor of Engineering, School of Engineering
description: Denton decided that Senior Engineering left too much spare time, and is assisting in propounding the intricacies of freshman mathematics. Ask him how he likes Lake Providence!

name: Dorroh, Will Edwin
motto: "By time and toil we sever what strength and rage could never."
major: Bachelor of Engineering, School of Engineering
description: "W. E." left us for a while to "learn" his profession and has come back to finish studying it. He has displayed his constructive tendencies in successfully engineering his way into the heart of a fair co-ed.

name: Ford, Howard Clay
motto: "A faultless body and a blameless mind."
major: Bachelor of Engineering, School of Engineering
description: "Henry," as he is best known, is too nice a fellow to be an engineer, as they are seldom "civil." He has a way with him that makes one like him first, last and always.

name: Johnson, William Edward
motto: "He thinks too much; such men are dangerous in their wisdom."
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING, SCHOOL OF ENGINEERING
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer of Engineering Class, '22
description: Johnson will make a regular engineer, as he has proved a conscientious student and is a real "shark" in his studies. Structures more amazing than our air castles will hardly be beyond him.

name: Lewis, Hugh Southerland
motto: "My heart is whole, my fancy free; run on, co-eds, don't bother me."
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING, SCHOOL OF ENGINEERING
description: It will probably never be explained why the most handsome man in school is making engineering his life work. But if he is as good as he looks, the profession will experience wonderful advances when he leaves us to join its ranks.

name: Mabus, Raymond E.
motto: "Cheerfulness and content are famous preservers of good looks."
major: BACHELOR OF ENGINEERING, SCHOOL OF ENGINEERING
description: It seems that Mabus is the, if not the only, social lion of his class. But don't think less of him for that; it will come in mighty well when he wants to land the big jobs he intends to.

name: Prados, Gustave Olivier
motto: "O birds that warble as the day goes by, Sing sweetly; twice my love hath smiled on me,"
major: Bachelor of Engineering, School of Engineering
description: We hesitate to mention anything about Prados, as he is so well known already. But if "Gus" is as successful in his profession as he's been in another way—well, we'll worry over someone else's future.

name: Thompson, Francis Thornton
motto: "Rises early, studies late, Works all day with books and slate."
major: Bachelor of Engineering, School of Engineering
description: "Themp" has given every aspiring member of his class a fight for first place, and has already seen what engineering is like with Uncle Sam in Montana. Ole Miss is glad to claim him.

name: Webb, Willie Ticer
motto: "Minds that have nothing to confer Find little to perceive."
major: Bachelor of Engineering, School of Engineering
description: More than one of his fellow students will miss Webb's cheerfulness and his amiable smile. It comes to us, too, that, aside from his engineering accomplishments, he intends to publish a new book of original jokes. We'll take four copies!

name: Barnhill, Milton Richard
motto: "As merry as the day is long."
description: "Bunny" isn't so little that he can't snore loud enough to keep the neighborhood awake. When he meets chemistry the teacher always fails to call the roll. "Hard Luck," "Bunny."

name: Bethany, Phinis Ky
motto: "A man he seems of cheerful yesterdays."
description: We all know that "Ky" will be as successful as any of the class. He is well liked by all the students and takes part in all activities. We shall all miss him.

name: Brown, Newton
motto: "The noblest study of mankind is woman."
clubs: Entertainment Committee, Y. M. C. A.
description: Of course everybody knows that "Deacon" will be married before long. He has a good start, and he says she is willing. He hasn't had time to count the love letters he has received. Look out, "Deacon," you'll be kidnapped before long!

name: Chandler, Wade B.
motto: "Cares not a pin what they say or may say."
description: He is quiet in a crowd, but I can't say what would happen when you get him alone. Ask her! A good student and popular with his classmates. We can look for big things to happen in the forthcoming years.

name: Curtis, G. W.
motto: "I am not in the roll of common men."
description: Curtis is one that doesn't take life seriously, but lets each day take care of itself. He stands high in his classes and takes part in all student activities. We know success is his.

name: Dees, James Obregon
motto: "Silence, more musical than any song."
description: He says that he is not a preacher if he does look like one. A good, all-round student, he says he is going to make "em" hop when he blows into town with his terrible line.

name: Featherston, Cleveland Beasley
motto: "Toil is the lot of all, but none for me."
clubs: Chairman of Employment Committee and Member Executive Committee, '21-'22
description: "Clive" says he wants some of his board money back, as he hasn't met breakfast but once this year, and he's such a shark in chemistry that he doesn't ever have to meet the class. Clive doesn't slight the path that leads toward the "Coop" either.

name: Hagood, Charles Lee
motto: "Thy face the index to a noble mind."
description: He has been with us for four years, and too much can't be said for him. He is an all-round Ole Miss man, participates in all activities, and can be called upon to do his part in any phase of the school life. He has held several offices of distinction.

name: Henderson, Hiram Willis
motto: "I would rather excel others in knowledge than power."
description: When he announces a written and ups and says he "knows his stuff," you had better beware. He has an "A" on his mind. One of the best students of the class, good-natured, and a gem in the hearts of those who know him.

name: Howell, James W.
motto: "The man who knows he knows, he knows."
description: Howell has been with us only two years, but he is a regular fellow, and we know that achievement is his middle name. We hate to see him go.

name: Jackson, Ernest Houston
motto: "See how doth the busy bee improve each shining hour."
description: "Jack" will be remembered when he has gone out to fight his battle to win. We know he will, as "Jack" says things don't get too difficult for him. One of the "gang," and a mighty important member.

name: Johnson, Wilson Hartwell
motto: "Things are bound to happen—why worry; everything comes to him who waits—why hurry?"
description: Brooksville lost a good man and Ole Miss gained one when "Happy" came to the University. A fine student, always willing to do his part in student activities. We wish him the very best in life.

name: Kent, George Spivey
motto: "Fraught with learning."
description: "Spivey" says that some of these days he intends to be a pharmacist. He is only learning how to do it now. One of the steadiest in the class and a regular lady's man. Watch your step, kid!

name: Lane, Roy L.
motto: "I had rather have a fool to make me merry than experience to make me sad."
description: He can't decide whether he should be a clown or a pharmacist, so we think he is taking a course in both. A jolly, all-round good sport.

name: Leggett, Lesley L.
motto: "There are two sides to every question—mine and the wrong side."
description: Leggett has been at Ole Miss two years, but everybody knows him. He has a big heart and a place in it for everybody. He is ambitious, care-free, clever, and a splendid lad who will rapidly mount the rungs of the ladder of success. We wish him luck in life.

name: Lyerly, Frank Griffin
motto: "Who mixed reason with stories, and wisdom with jokes."
description: When he isn't acting in the capacity of football manager, "Skipper" studies pharmacy enough to keep well up with his class. "Skipper's" chief delight is in telling one of his unique collection of navy jokes.

name: Pitchford, Mary Howard
motto: "And looks commercing with the skies, thy rapt soul sitting in thine eyes."
description: "Howard" has thoroughly convinced herself that she doesn't like men, and so firmly believes it that she is trying to convince the rest of us of it. So, to please her, we shall predict a meteoric career. However, some men don't stop for a career.

name: Rape, Cyrus Amis
motto: "Persuasion tips his tongue whene'er he talks."
clubs: Secretary-Treasurer Ole Miss Board of Control, '22
description: Cy is a little man, but sometimes he can be so big! All the seniors say that's so after he adopted the greeting, "Got that ten dollars yet?" A number of the men on the basketball floor can say so, too, and emphatically.

name: Seale, Sam H.
motto: "Perseverance conquers all things."
description: When the chemistry sharks are advertised for, "Sam" will no doubt be the people's choice, as the next book to be written will be his, "How I Learned Organic Chemistry." A good, all-round student, well liked by all. Luck to you, Sam!
